etymology.description: 詞(詞) (cí, "word; term; expression") and 彙(匯) (huì, "to collect; collection").
etymology.interpretation: 詞(詞) (cí, "word; term; expression") represents the basic linguistic unit, a word or a term. 彙(匯) (huì, "to collect; collection") denotes the gathering or grouping of items. Together, 詞彙 (cíhuì, "vocabulary") describes a collection or compilation of words and expressions, much like a word list or glossary that groups individual words into a comprehensive set.

examples.chinese: 这个学生的词汇量很大(這個學生的詞彙量很大)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xuéshēng de cíhuì liàng hěn dà.
examples.english: This student has a large vocabulary.

examples.chinese: 老师要求我们每天学习新词汇(老師要求我們每天學習新詞彙)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī yāoqiú wǒmen měitiān xuéxí xīn cíhuì.
examples.english: The teacher requires us to learn new vocabulary every day.

examples.chinese: 提高词汇量可以帮助你更好地表达自己(提高詞彙量可以幫助你更好地表達自己)。
examples.pinyin: Tígāo cíhuì liàng kěyǐ bāngzhù nǐ gèng hǎo de biǎodá zìjǐ.
examples.english: Improving your vocabulary can help you express yourself better.
